Items.Title: 编程：爱好还是工作？ | Programming: Hobby or Job?
Items.Description:
我正在浏览我的 dev.to 提要，我看到了许多与编程工作相关的帖子。

我 11 岁时开始编码，从那以后我一直把它作为一种爱好。

你编程是为了工作还是爱好？

皮埃尔-亨利·索里亚✨ - Apr 8 4月8日

Michael Tharrington (he/him) - Michael Tharrington (他/他) - Apr 25 4月25日



原文:

I was scrolling through my dev.to feed, and I saw many posts relating to programming jobs.

I started coding when I was 11, and I’ve been using it as a hobby since.

Do you program for a job or as a hobby?

Michael Tharrington (he/him) - May 2

Pierre-Henry Soria ✨ - Apr 8

Michael Tharrington (he/him) - Apr 25

Items.Title: Appwrite 社区报告 #5 | Appwrite Community Report #5
Items.Description:
用于Appwrite的haimantika mitra

发表于大家好！ 来自 Appwrite 团队的问候👋

我们带着每周社区报告回来了，与大家分享 Appwrite 一直在做的事情！ 这里先睹为快！ 这一次，我们有 OSS 基金公告、Appwrite x DEV Hackathon 进度更新、错误修复、新功能、30 天的 Appwrite 等等！ 请继续关注到最后😉

我们听取您的反馈并尝试每周实施！ 以下是我们本周解决的问题：

授权跳过方法的改进
  • 使用 MongoDB 适配器更新 Utopia/数据库
  • 正在使用 Hashing API 进行工作，这将帮助您轻松地将用户导入/导出到/从其他平台。
  • 修复 Deno 的“listAttributes”类型
  • 受邀加入团队的用户现在出现在用户列表中

    • 说说Appwrite的权限
  • 多项功能改进

    • 弗拉基米尔·伊万年科—— May 7 5月7日



    原文:

    haimantika mitra for Appwrite

    Posted on May 8

    Hello everyone! Greetings from team Appwrite 👋

    We are back with our weekly community report, to share with you all what Appwrite has been doing! 🤩

    Here’s a sneak peek! 👀

    TLDR; This time around we have OSS Fund Announcement, Appwrite x DEV Hackathon progress updates, bug fixes, new features, 30 days of Appwrite, and a lot more! Stay tuned till the end 😉

    📢 What’s new:

    • Appwrite just announced their community initiative “OSS Fund” worth $50,000 to support open-source project maintainers! 😮 We cannot wait to see your amazing projects! 🤩

    Appwrite provides web and mobile developers with a set of easy-to-use and integrate REST APIs to manage their core backend needs.

  • Appwrite hack ends soon, make sure you drop your solutions before the deadline ⏰

    • P.S - Exclusive swags await every participant! 😉

    ✅ Issues solved:

    We hear your feedback and try to implement them every week! Here are the issues we’ve fixed this week:

    ⚒️ What we’re currently working on:

    • Improvement of authorization skip method
    • Updates on Utopia/database with MongoDB adapter
    • Work-in-progress with Hashing API, that will help you import/export users to/from other platforms effortlessly.
    • Fixing type of ‘listAttributes’ for Deno
    • Python function create_deployment now accepts boolean property
    • Users invited to the team now appear in users list

    🗣 Discussions:

    • Let’s talk about Appwrite’s permissions
    • Multiple function improvements

    📘 Resources:

    Vladimir Ivanenko - May 7
Items.Author: haimantika

Items.Title: 我讨厌 React JS，但现在我不能没有它…… | I hated React JS, but nowadays I can’t live without it…
Items.Description:
六个月前，我讨厌 React JS 如此冗长，我想，“React 是最糟糕的”，但突然发生了一个奇迹，迫使我开始爱上 React JS。 你可能认为奇迹不是真的，但读完这篇你就会开始相信它们……

在学习前端 Js 框架的最初几天，我主要品尝了两个前端框架； 但是这两个框架都缺少一些东西，那就是简单。

另外，我无法修复它，也许是因为社区不够大。

而且，VueJS 的问题是它的代码太丑了，没有纯语法。 出于这个原因，我不得不离开 Vue Js。 此外，VueJs 刚刚成长，我需要服务器端渲染，而 Vue3 在当时是不可能的。

最后，关键时刻； 我不得不选择 React JS，因为我别无选择，你可能会说我会尝尝 Angular，但我对 Angular 洗脑了，以至于我没有机会检查 Angular。 没有人喜欢 Angular，至少在我的圈子里是这样！

我在 YouTube 上搜索了 React JS 的教程，发现它使用起来非常简单。 确实，它的工作既冗长又笨重，但使用起来很简单。 老实说，这很简单。 它也很容易学习！

我尝试在不看任何教程的情况下使用 React JS 制作我自己的 Web 应用程序，它成功了，我能够制作我自己的基于 React 的 Web 应用程序而无需看教程。 我觉得奇迹刚刚发生。 嗯，我很擅长用 Django 制作网络应用程序，所以也许这对我有帮助。 另外，我已经有两个基于 Django 的 web 应用程序在生产中给我带来了被动收入，所以我对编程或 webdev 并不陌生，我只是一个前端 JS 框架的新手。

所以，我决定现在就留在 React JS，和 React Js 一起过上幸福的生活直到永远。

此外，现在我正在将基于 Django 的 webapps 迁移到 NextJs/Django React Framwork Ba​​sed webapps。

所以学习它并成为 React JS 的倡导者。 但是 ReactJS 绝对不是初学者友好的，不是吗？

感谢您使我们的网络社区成为一个拥有 1.3K 成员的大家庭，伙计们，请跟上 Josh！

祝你有美好的一天，请不要讨厌任何其他 JS 框架，它们不是我喜欢的类型。



原文:

Six months ago I hated React JS for being so lengthy, I was like, “React Is the Worst”, but suddenly a miracle happened that forced me to start loving React JS. You might think miracles are not real, but after reading this you’ll start believing in them…

In the initial days of learning frontend Js frameworks, I tasted mainly two frontend frameworks; VueJS and Svelte. But there was something missing in both frameworks, that was simplicity.

React JS is undoubtedly more lengthy and bulky (while working) than Svelte or Vue Js. Svelte being the shortest syntax-ed Javascript framework, but still Svelte was not my type.

Svelte did a pretty significant Job with SvelteKit (like NextJs of ReactJs) but the issue was the two CSS files (import ./*.css) of separate pages being mixed up, and I had an uncozy feeling with that. Also, I wasn’t able to fix it, maybe that’s because the community wasn’t that huge enough.

And, the problem with VueJS is that its code is so ugly, that it has no pure syntax. For that reason, I have to leave Vue Js. Also, VueJs was just growing and I need server side-rendering, and with Vue3 that wasn’t possible at that time.

Finally, the moment of truth; I had to choose React JS, ’cause I had no choice, you might say I would have tasted Angular, but I was such brain-washed about Angular that there was no chance for me to check out Angular. No one likes Angular, at least in my circle!

I searched tutorials for React JS on YouTube, and I found it pretty simple to use. Indeed, it was lengthy and bulky to work, but it’s simple to use. Honestly, it was simple. It was easy to learn as well!

I tried making my own web apps with React JS without watching any tutorial, and it worked, I was able to make my own React Based webapp without watching tutorials. I felt like a miracle just happened. Well, I was pretty good at making web apps with Django, so maybe that helped there. Also, I had already two Django-based web apps on the production which are giving me passive income, so I wasn’t new at programming or webdev, I was just new a frontend JS frameworks.

So, I have decided to stay with React JS now, and live a happy life with React Js till eternity.

Also, nowadays I am migrating my Django Based webapps to NextJs/Django React Framwork Based webapps.

React JS is simple, flexible and easy to learn; so learn it and be an advocate of React JS. But ReactJS is definitely not beginner friendly, or is it?

Thanks for making our web community a family of 1.3K Members, Keep Up The Josh, guys!

Have a good day, please don’t hate any other JS frameworks, they’re just not my type.
Items.Author: developerbishwas

Items.Title: 将 WordPress 应用程序部署到 Amazon Lightsail | Deploy WordPress App to Amazon Lightsail
Items.Description:
与大多数其他服务不同，它具有固定的每月定价，因此您不必担心每月的巨额账单。

它对WordPress 、Magento、Joomla、Prestashop、Drupal、Ghost 和 Django 应用程序提供开箱即用的支持。

它提供了预配置的堆栈，例如 LAMP、NGINX、MEAN 和 Node.js。 只需单击几下，您还可以启动预配置的 Plesk、WHM 和 cPanel。

但这还不是全部。 您可以启动任何基于 Linux 和 Windows 操作系统的实例。 我个人最喜欢的是Ubuntu和Amazon Linux 2 。

先决条件

需要一个有效的 AWS 账户来部署 WP 应用程序。 如果您没有，只需从此处创建一个。 在开始部署您的应用程序之前，请阅读有关Amazon Lightsail 定价和免费套餐的信息。 不用担心，我会尽量将所有内容都保留在免费套餐内。 在撰写本文时，您可以免费运行一个 Lightsail 实例 3 个月。

安装 WordPress 应用程序

如果尚未登录，请登录您的 AWS 账户。 转到Lightsail 主页。 你应该看到这样的页面：

你看到这个了吗？ 我们走吧🏃‍♂️

创建实例

  1. 现在单击大的创建实例按钮。 如果您没有看到，请按照以下说明操作：

  2. 让我们选择Instance location 、 platform和blueprint ：
    我建议选择离客户更近的位置。

    不是多站点之一。

  • 现在向下滚动到选择您的实例计划部分。 我选择了一个免费套餐并且只有一个实例。 这是我的设置：

  • 最后滚动到页面底部并单击Create instance按钮。 等待状态变为Running 。 恭喜，您的实例已启动🚀

    • 分配静态 IP（可选）

    每次停止和启动实例时都会发生变化。 当我启动实例时，我的公共 IP 是这样的：

    停止并启动实例后，它获得了一个新的公共 IP 地址：

    我们必须解决这个问题。 因此，让我们创建并分配一个静态 IP ：

    1. 单击您的应用程序实例名称以查看您的实例的详细信息。
    2. 单击+ 从此处创建静态 IP ：
    3. 添加一个有意义的名称，然后单击创建： 您需要为未附加到正在运行的实例的静态 IP 付费。

    使用 SSH 连接到实例

    您可以通过两种方式连接到实例：

    使用浏览器

    这是最简单的。 转到“连接”选项卡，然后单击“使用 SSH 连接”：

    使用 SSH 客户端

    1. 从这里下载 SSH 密钥：
    2. 如果您使用 PuTTY，您可以按照本教程进行操作。
    3. 如果您在 macOS 或 Linux 中使用终端，请在下载 SSH 密钥的位置运行此命令。
     # Run for the first time to fix the permission chmod 400 <path/to/your-lightsail-ssh-key>.pem # Connect to your Lightsail instance # Username and Public IP can be found in "Connect" tab ssh -i "<path/to/your-lightsail-key>.pem" <username>@<public_ip>

    获取管理员用户名和密码

    1. 您可以从“连接”选项卡中获取用户名。 用户名是此堆栈的用户。
    2. 要获取密码，请登录 SSH 并运行此命令
    cat bitnami_application_password

    1. 现在打开浏览器选项卡并转到http://<public_ip>/wp-admin 。 在上面输入用户名和密码。 您应该像我一样看到 WP Admin 仪表板。

    添加域

    1. 转到主页。 单击网络选项卡，然后单击创建 DNS 区域按钮
    2. 添加您的域名。 滚动到底部并单击创建 DNS 区域按钮
    3. 在您的域控制面板中添加这些名称服务器记录（例如 Namecheap、GoDaddy）。 你的记录可能与我的不同
    4. 现在到了好的部分。 单击+ 添加记录按钮。 添加您的实例并单击对勾图标进行保存。 现在更新wp_options表的siteurl和home行中的域名。

    导入现有应用

    导入数据库

    您可能有一个现有的数据库。 您可以将数据库导入 Lightsail 实例。 您可以使用 TablePlus、MySQL Workbench 等数据库客户端应用程序，甚至只是终端。

    您的数据库凭据存在于wp-config.php文件中。 通过 SSH 连接到您的实例并运行以下命令：

     cat stack/wordpress/wp-config.php

    使用数据库凭证连接数据库，并将localhost替换为实例的公共 IP。 通过 SSH 连接到您的数据库（推荐）或在此处添加3306端口：

    导入现有文件

    位于stack/wordpress中的应用程序文件夹。 您可以通过两种方式上传文件：

    1. 使用相同的 SSH 凭据上传文件。
    2. 使用以下命令安装 git：

    现在使用 git clone/pull 命令从 git 中提取文件。

    结论

    这只是将 WordPress 应用程序部署到 Amazon Lightsail 实例的第一步。 你可以做的还有很多。 欢迎您提出问题、给我反馈或任何改进要求。



    原文:

    AWS Lightsail can be the easiest entry to the AWS Cloud. It has fixed monthly pricing unlike most other services, so you don't have to worry about a huge monthly bill.

    Lightsail is really great for small to medium apps. It has out-of-the-box support for WordPress, Magento, Joomla, Prestashop, Drupal, Ghost, and Django apps.

    It provides pre-configured stacks like LAMP, NGINX, MEAN, and Node.js. You can also launch pre-configured Plesk, WHM & cPanel in just a few clicks.

    But that's not all. You can launch any Linux-based and Windows OS instance. My personal favorites are Ubuntu and Amazon Linux 2.

    Prerequisites

    Need a valid AWS account to deploy the WP app. If you don't have one, just create one from here. Before starting to deploy your app read about Amazon Lightsail Pricing and Free-Tier. Don't worry, I will try to keep everything within the free tier. At this time of writing, you can run a Lightsail instance for free for 3 months.

    Install WordPress App

    Login to your AWS account if not already. Go to Lightsail homepage. You should see a page like this:

    Are you seeing this? Let's go 🏃‍♂️

    Create Instance

    1. Now click on the big Create Instance button. If you don't see one, follow this:

    2. Let's choose Instance location, platform, and blueprint:
      A. Click the link to change location and availability zone (AZ) if not what you want. I recommend selecting a location closer to your customers.

      B. Choose the Linux/Unix platform.


      C. Select the Apps + OS from the blueprint.
      D. Now choose WordPress from the app list. Not the multisite one.

  • Now scroll down to Choose your instance plan section. I chose a free-tier plan and only one instance. Here is my setup:

  • Finally scroll to the bottom of the page and click the Create instance button. Wait for the status to become Running. Congratulations, your instance has launched 🚀

    • Assign a Static IP (optional)

    AWS Lightsail Instance comes with public (and private) IP. Which changes every time Stop and Start the instance. My Public IP was this when I launched the instance:

    After Stop and Start the instance, it got a new Public IP address:

    We have to fix that. So, let's create and assign a Static IP:

    1. Click on your application instance name to see the details of your instance.
    2. Click on + Create static IP from here:
    3. Add a meaningful name and click Create: > ⚠️ Warning: You are allowed to assign 5 static IPs for free. You will be charged for static IP that is not attached to a running instance.

    Connect to the instance using SSH

    You can connect to an instance in two ways:

    Using browser

    That's the easiest. Go to the Connect tab and click Connect using SSH:

    Using SSH Client

    1. Download the SSH key from here:
    2. If you are using PuTTY you can follow this tutorial.
    3. If you are using a terminal in macOS or Linux, run this command on where you downloaded the SSH key. 
    # Run for the first time to fix the permission
chmod 400 <path/to/your-lightsail-ssh-key>.pem # Connect to your Lightsail instance
# Username and Public IP can be found in "Connect" tab
ssh -i "<path/to/your-lightsail-key>.pem" <username>@<public_ip>

    Get Admin Username and Password

    1. You can get the username from the Connect tab. The username is user for this stack.
    2. To get the password, log in to SSH and run this command 
    cat bitnami_application_password

    1. Now open a browser tab and go to http://<public_ip>/wp-admin. Enter username and password above. You should see the WP Admin dashboard like me.

    Add a Domain

    1. Go to the Home page. Click on the Networking tab and then hit Create DNS zone button
    2. Add your domain name. Scroll to the bottom and click the Create DNS zone button
    3. Add these name server records in your domain control panel (ex. Namecheap, GoDaddy). You might have different records than mine
    4. Now to the good part. Click + Add Record button. Add your instance and click on tick icon to save. > ⚠️ It may take few minutes to days to propagate DNS records.
    5. Now update the domain name in siteurl and home rows of wp_options table.

    Import Existing App

    Import Database

    You may have an existing database. You can import your database to the Lightsail instance. You can use a Database client app like TablePlus, MySQL Workbench, or even just the terminal.

    Your database credentials exist in wp-config.php file. Connect to your instance via SSH and run this command:

    cat stack/wordpress/wp-config.php

    Use DB credentials to connect to DB, and replace localhost with the instance's public IP. Connect to your DB via SSH (recommended) or add the 3306 port here:

    ℹ️ Don't forget to change the domain name in siteurl and home rows of wp_options table.

    Import existing files

    Application folder located in stack/wordpress. You can upload files in two ways:

    1. SFTP Client: You can use SFTP clients like FileZilla or Transmit to upload files. Use the same SSH credential to upload files.
    2. Git: Connect to your instance via SSH. Install git by using this command:

    Now pull your files from git using the git clone/pull command.

    Conclusion

    This is just the first step toward deploying your WordPress app to Amazon Lightsail instance. There are lot more you can do. You are welcome to ask your questions, give me feedback, or any improvement request.
Items.Author: harunrrayhan

Items.Title: 没有 Android Studio 的 Quick React Native Android 设置 (2022) | Quick React Native Android Set Up without Android Studio (2022)
Items.Description:
官方 React Native 指南提供安装 Android Studio，我不喜欢。 但如果涉及到 React Native，Android 和 iOS 一样重要。 这是一个不使用 Android Studio 的 React Native 项目的快速设置指南。

节点

首先，您需要安装 Node 和 Watchman。 我建议使用Homebrew：

 brew install node brew install watchman

爪哇

与使用基于 Intel 的 JDK 相比，这将确保您在 M1 Mac 上的构建速度更快。

使用以下命令验证安装：

安卓 SDK

转到Android Studio 网站并下载命令行工具：

在根目录下创建一个文件夹：

 /Library/Android/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/

将下载的cmdline-tools文件夹重命名为latest并将其放入创建的文件夹中。 完整路径应该是：

 /Library/Android/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/...

在~/.bash_profile或~/.bashrc （如果你使用 zsh 则~/.zprofile或~/.zshrc /.zshrc ）配置文件中设置环境变量：

 export ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/Library/Android/android-sdk export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/emulator export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/platform-tools

转到/Library/Android/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin 。 现在您可以使用sdkmanager安装所需的 SDK 版本。 我将安装以下内容：

 ./sdkmanager "platforms;android-23" "build-tools;23.0.1" "add-ons;addon-google_apis-google-23"

您可以在此处找到有关如何使用sdkmanager的更多信息。

我将使用Genymotion。 作为模拟器。 它是免费且易于使用的。

您可以使用 Homebrew 下载它：

 brew install --cask genymotion

设置我们在 Genymotion 上下载的自定义 Android SDK。 转到设置 → ADB，将值设置为：

使用您的 Android 版本添加新的虚拟设备并运行它。

使用以下命令运行应用程序：

就是这样。 超级快。 如您所见，您无需下载 Android Studio 即可使用 Android 模拟器运行您的应用程序。 感谢阅读🙌🙌🙌



原文:

Official React Native guide offers installing Android Studio, which I prefer not. But Android is important as iOS if it comes to React Native. Here is a quick set up guide for your React Native project without using Android Studio.

Node

First of all, you will need to install Node and Watchman. I recommend using Homebrew:

brew install node
brew install watchman

Java

React Native team recommends installing the OpenJDK distribution called Azul Zulu:

brew tap homebrew/cask-versions
brew install --cask zulu11

The Zulu OpenJDK distribution offers JDKs for both Intel and M1 Macs. This will make sure your build are faster on M1 Macs compared to using an Intel-based JDK.

Verify the installation with the following command:

Android SDK

Go to Android Studio website and download the Command line tools:

Create a folder under the root:

/Library/Android/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/

Rename the downloaded cmdline-tools folder to latest and put it inside created folder. The full path should be:

/Library/Android/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/...

Set up environment variables in your ~/.bash_profile or ~/.bashrc (if you are using zsh then ~/.zprofile or ~/.zshrc) config file:

export ANDROID_SDK_ROOT=/Library/Android/android-sdk
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/emulator
export PATH=$PATH:$ANDROID_SDK_ROOT/platform-tools

Go to /Library/Android/android-sdk/cmdline-tools/latest/bin. Now you can install the SDK version you want using sdkmanager. I'll install the following:

./sdkmanager "platforms;android-23" "build-tools;23.0.1" "add-ons;addon-google_apis-google-23"

More info on how to use sdkmanager you can find here.

Genymotion

I'll use Genymotion. as an emulator. It's free and easy to use.

You can download it with Homebrew:

brew install --cask genymotion

Set the custom Android SDK we've downloaded on Genymotion. Go to Settings → ADB, set the value to:

Add new virtual device with your Android version and run it.

Run the application with the following command:

That's it. Super-quick. As you can see you don't need to download Android Studio to run your app using Android emulator. Thanks for reading 🙌🙌🙌

Items.Title: 混合任务搜索别名 | Mix task to search for alias name
Items.Description:
苏珊特·巴杰拉查亚

发表于我最近不得不从事一个使用多别名的项目

在这样的项目中搜索很困难，所以我编写了一个混合任务来查找别名，然后在终端上打印文件和行号。

安东尼冈萨雷斯 - 乔纳森·杨 - 安东尼冈萨雷斯 - 圣地亚哥卡多纳 - Apr 27 '21 21 年 4 月 27 日

Sushant Bajracharya

Searcing is difficult on project like this so I wrote a mix task that would look for the alias name and then print the file and line number on the terminal.

Items.Title: NLP@AWS 时事通讯 05/2022 | NLP@AWS Newsletter 05/2022
Items.Description:
你好世界。 这是 AWS 自然语言处理 (NLP) 月刊，涵盖 AWS 与 NLP 相关的所有内容。 随时发表评论并在您的社交网络上分享。

最近有一个 AWS/Hugging Face 联合研讨会，现在可以在YouTube上获得关于如何“使用知识蒸馏和 AWS 推理加速 BERT 推理”的内容

该研讨会将引导您通过应用知识蒸馏将大型模型压缩为小型模型，然后从小型模型压缩为可在 AWS Inferentia 上运行的优化神经元模型。 减小模型尺寸和 Inferentia 定制芯片的推理改进相结合，将延迟从 100 毫秒以上减少到 5 毫秒以上——提高了 20 倍！

研讨会涵盖：

  • 以 BERT-large 作为教师和 MiniLM 作为学生应用知识蒸馏
  • 使用 AWS Neuron 为 AWS Inferentia 编译 Hugging Face Transformer 模型
  • 将提炼和优化的模型部署到 Amazon SageMaker 以进行生产级快速推理

在 Hugging Face YouTube频道上有一组很棒的演示文稿和教程，非常值得深入挖掘后面的目录并订阅新视频。

迄今为止，Searchmetrics 已帮助麦肯锡公司、Lowe's 和 AXA 等 1,000 多家公司在竞争激烈的搜索领域找到了优势。

这篇文章概述了 Searchmetrics 如何构建一个人工智能解决方案，通过使用 Amazon SageMaker 及其与 Hugging Face 的原生集成，自动查找任何给定主题的相关搜索关键字，从而将人力效率提高 20%。

在新闻中：从书面描述生成插图

它使用一种称为“扩散”的过程，该过程从随机点的图案开始，并在识别图像的特定方面时逐渐改变该图案朝向图像。 创建的图像与您的预期一致，但允许请求指定绘图的样式，例如下面的两个图像是“宇航员骑马”。 一个请求以“铅笔画”结尾，另一个请求以“逼真的风格”结尾。

其他示例允许提出更详细和微妙的要求，例如“浣熊宇航员，他的头盔玻璃上反射着宇宙，梦见星星”提供了一个似乎传达了所要求的情感的图像，但也成功地组成了多个图像同时保持比例。

更多详细信息可以在 OpenAI博客文章中找到，该文章宣布了 DALL·E 2 基础研究论文，即使用 CLIP Latents 生成分层文本条件图像。

在新闻中：人工智能终于开了个玩笑

随着自然语言处理的不断发展，它已经超越了检测实体、意图或简单的情绪，现在它可以“理解”并解释一个笑话：

虽然这些笑话可能不是最好的（创造它们的数据科学家可能会更好地坚持日常工作），但模型对笑话的解释导致了有趣的可能性。 虽然仍然是一个非常重要的研究项目，但在客户服务聊天机器人中应用它可以产生比字面意思理解讽刺客户的评论更好的结果。

该模型本身建立在 Google 的 Pathways 语言模型 (PaLM) 之上，该模型已扩展到 5400 亿个参数，以实现这种洞察力。

阅读原始源博客文章或通过阅读PaLM 论文更深入地了解。

银行和金融机构每周审查数千个信贷申请，这篇博客文章概述了金融机构如何使用 AWS 提供的一系列人工智能服务快速有效地加快流程、降低成本并通过更快的决策提供更好的客户体验。 虽然给出的场景来自金融服务领域，但以银行对账单、水电费等形式提供文件和证据的需求适用于许多业务流程，例如施工许可、工作申请或政府流程，例如护照申请。
这篇博文介绍了使用 Amazon Connect、Amazon Lex 和 Amazon Textract 以及其他无服务器功能（例如 Amazon API Gateway 和 AWS Lambda）来实现无服务器模型，以快速部署功能正常的解决方案。

许多在 AWS 上构建无服务器 Web 应用程序的人都会熟悉上述解决方案模式，这篇文章将自然语言处理和智能文档处理功能集成到这样的应用程序中。

识别实体是许多智能文档处理工作流的关键部分，无论是创建一组带标签的文档、构建索引还是触发业务流程。 过去，您只能在纯文本文档上使用 Amazon Comprehend，因此，如果您想分析 PDF 或 Word 文档（通常情况下），您将首先转换为纯文本。 这不仅是一个额外的步骤，还意味着您丢失了与文档中的位置和布局相关的其他属性，这些属性可用于提高识别度。 去年，Amazon Comprehend 宣布支持从原生文档格式中提取自定义实体，并支持使用Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth对训练文档进行注释。

这篇文章讨论了配置 Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth 的过程
直接注释 PDF 文档，构建自定义实体模型并使用附加的结构上下文为模型提供更多信息，以帮助识别文档​​中的相关实体。

如今，自动语音识别 (ASR) 似乎无处不在，无论是 Alexa 或 Siri 等语音控制助手，还是呼叫中心转录、语音翻译等等。 虽然使用预构建的 AI 服务（例如Amazon Transcribe ）可以有效处理许多场景，但任何 NLP 从业者都可以使用多种工具总是好的。 一个例子是 Hugging Face Wav2Vec2 模型，它使用连接主义时间分类 (CTC) 算法，因为 CTC 是一种基于字符的算法，它能够生成具有记录的逐字符序列的输出，请参阅使用 CTC 进行序列建模作为背景。

这篇博文描述了在 SageMaker 上部署端到端 Wav2Vec2 解决方案，下图提供了解决方案工作流程的高级视图。

本文中概述的方法可用于为您自己的数据集微调 Wav2Vec2 模型。

这篇博文介绍了使用 SageMaker Serverless Inference 部署 Hugging Face 模型的步骤，继上个月在 re:Invent 2021 上宣布推出 GA 之后。

无服务器推理加入了现有的实时推理端点、批量转换和异步推理的 SageMaker 部署模型，使您可以轻松部署和扩展 ML 模型。


除了描述将许多流行的 Hugging Face 模型部署到无服务器推理的过程之外，该帖子还分析了与 ml.t2.medium 实例上的实时端点相比的相对成本，展示了使用以下模型可以节省的成本间歇性或低水平的访问。



原文:

Hello world. This is the monthly AWS Natural Language Processing(NLP) newsletter covering everything related to NLP at AWS. Feel free to leave comments & share it on your social network.

Joint AWS / Hugging Face Workshop

There was a recent joint AWS/Hugging Face Workshop, now available on YouTube on how to ‘Accelerate BERT Inference with Knowledge Distillation & AWS Inferentia’

The workshop takes you through applying knowledge distillation to compress a large model to a small model, and then from the small model to an optimized neuron model ready to run on AWS Inferentia. The combination of reducing the model size and the Inference improvements from Inferentia custom silicon yielded a reduction of latency from 100ms+ to 5ms+ - a 20x improvement!

The workshop covered:

  • Applying knowledge-distillation with BERT-large as teacher and MiniLM as student
  • Compiling Hugging Face Transformer model with AWS Neuron for AWS Inferentia
  • Deploying the distilled & optimized model to Amazon SageMaker for production-grade fast inference

There is a great set of presentations and tutorials On the Hugging Face YouTube channel well worth digging into the back catalogue and subscribing for new videos.

NLP Customer Success Stories

How Searchmetrics uses Amazon SageMaker to automatically find relevant keywords and make their human analysts 20% faster

Searchmetrics is a global provider of search data, software, and consulting solutions, helping customers turn search data into unique business insights. To date, Searchmetrics has helped more than 1,000 companies such as McKinsey & Company, Lowe’s, and AXA find an advantage in the hyper-competitive search landscape.

In 2021, Searchmetrics turned to AWS to help with artificial intelligence (AI) usage to further improve their search insights capabilities.

This post outlines how Searchmetrics built an AI solution that increased the efficiency of its human workforce by 20% by automatically finding relevant search keywords for any given topic, using Amazon SageMaker and its native integration with Hugging Face.

In the News : Generating Illustrations from written description

OpenAI have announced DALL·E 2 a successor to the DALL·E system that they announced last year that can create realistic images and art from a description in natural language. DALL·E 2 has learned the relationship between images and the text used to describe them. It uses a process called “diffusion,” which starts with a pattern of random dots and gradually alters that pattern towards an image when it recognizes specific aspects of that image. The images created are consistent with what you would expect, but allow the request to specify the style of a drawing, for example the two images below are of ‘An astronaut riding a horse.’ with one request ending ‘as a pencil drawing’ and the other requesting ‘a photrealistic style’.

Other examples allow for more detailed and subtle requests to be made, for example ‘a raccoon astronaut with the cosmos reflecting on the glass of his helmet dreaming of the stars’ provides an image which seems to convey the emotion requested, but also successfully composes multiple images while retaining proportions.

More detail can be found on the OpenAI blog post announcing DALL·E 2 underlying research paper on Hierarchical Text-Conditional Image Generation with CLIP Latents.

In the News : AI finally gets the joke

As Natural Language Processing continues to evolve it has moved beyond detecting entities, intents or simple sentiments to now being at the point where it can now ‘understand’ and explain a joke:

While the jokes are perhaps not the best (the Data Scientists that created them may be better sticking to the day job) the model’s explanation of the joke leads to interesting possibilities. While still very much a research project , the application of this in customer service chatbots could for example yielding better results than taking the comments of a sarcastic customer too literally.

The model itself is built on Google’s Pathways Language Model (PaLM) which has scaled to 540 billion parameters in order to achieve this level of insight.

Read the original source blog post or dive even deeper by reading the PaLM paper.

NLP Posts from the AWS Machine Learning Blog

Build a virtual credit approval agent with Amazon Lex, Amazon Textract, and Amazon Connect

Banking and financial institutions review thousands of credit applications per week, this blog post outlines how financial institutions can quickly and effectively expedite the process, reduce cost, and provide better customer experience with faster decisions using the range of Artificial Intelligence services available from AWS. While the scenario given is from the financial services world, the need to provide documentation and evidence in the form of bank statements, utility bills etc. has applicability to a number of business processes, such as construction permits, job applications or government processes such as passport applications.
This post describes implementing a serverless model using Amazon Connect, Amazon Lex and Amazon Textract, along with other serverless capabilities such as Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda to rapidly deploy a functioning solution.

The solution pattern above will be familiar to many who build serverless web applications on AWS, this post brings to life integrating Natural Language Processing and Intelligent Document processing capabilities into such an app.

Build a custom entity recogniser for PDF documents using Amazon Comprehend

Identifying entities is a key part of many intelligent document processing workflows, whether it is to create a labelled set of documents, construct an index or trigger a business process. Amazon Comprehend provides the ability to not only for the generic entities supported by the service such as dates, person or location, but also custom entities related to your data set such as product codes or other business specific entities.

In the past you could only use Amazon Comprehend on plain text documents, so if you wanted to analyse a PDF or Word Document as is often the case you would first convert to plain text. As well as being an additional step, this also meant you lost additional attributes related to position and layout in the document that could be used to drive stronger recognition. Last year Amazon Comprehend support for extracting custom entities from native document formats was announced along with support for annotating training documents using Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth.

This post talks through the process of configuring Amazon SageMaker Ground Truth
to annotate PDF documents directly, to build a custom entity model and use the additional structural context to provide more information to the model to help identify the relevant entities within documents.

Fine-tune and deploy a Wav2Vec2 model for speech recognition with Hugging Face and Amazon SageMaker

Automatic speech recognition (ASR) seems to be everywhere these days, whether voice-controlled assistants such as Alexa or Siri, through to call centre transcriptions, voice translations and more. While many scenarios can be effectively handled utilising pre-build AI services such as Amazon Transcribe, it is always good for any NLP practitioner to have multiple tools at their disposal. One example is the Hugging Face Wav2Vec2 model which uses a Connectionist Temporal Classification (CTC) algorithm, as CTC is a character-based algorithm it has the capability to generate output with the character-by-character sequence of the recording see Sequence Modelling With CTC for a backgrounder.

This blog post describes a deployment of an end-to-end Wav2Vec2 solution on SageMaker, the following diagram provides a high-level view of the solution workflow.

The approach outlined int this post can be utilised to fine tune the Wav2Vec2 model for your own data sets.

Host Hugging Face transformer models using Amazon SageMaker Serverless Inference
 This blog post walks through the steps to deploy a Hugging Face model using SageMaker Serverless Inference, which following being announced at re:Invent 2021 went GA last month.

Serverless Inference joins the existing SageMaker deployment models of Real-time inference endpoints, Batch transform and Asynchronous inference, to make it easy for you to deploy and scale ML models.


As well as describing the process of deploying a number of popular Hugging Face models onto Serverless Inference, the post analyses the relative cost compared to a real time endpoint on an ml.t2.medium instance, demonstrating the savings that can be made for models with intermittent or low levels of access.
Items.Author: andrewtownhill

Items.Title: StackOverBot，一个用于搜索 StackOverflow 问题的 Discord 机器人 | StackOverBot, a Discord bot for searching StackOverflow questions
Items.Description:
在我们开始之前，我只想谈谈我与 python 的关系😄（ 直接跳到机器人）

与 Python 的关系

我使用 Python 已经快 10 年了，但我从未深入研究过它，当我只需要制作快速工具（如排序文件、处理数据、需要快速 QT 接口的工具）时，我会谨慎使用它。 尽管我已经做了很多小项目，但我认为这些年来我认为自己是 Python 的初学者。

为什么是 StackOverflow 机器人？

制作 Discord 机器人有点挑战，因为我的许多朋友已经做过了。 我选择 StackOverflow 是因为StackExchange/StackOverflow API基本上只是在玩 URL 变量并发出发回 JSON 的 Web 请求。

机器人本身

该机器人是使用 Python 3.9 和Pycord 制作的
项目的 Github

机器人发送的问题

搜索问题的命令

该机器人目前可以接受 4 个输入

  • 通过与⏭做出反应，机器人会向您展示下一个问题！
    您最多可以查看 5 个问题

    待办事项：

    • 添加最大选项
    • 添加以最小和最大写日期的可能性，以便您可以在创建或活动中使用它

    容器化我的机器人🐋

    我的机器人可以使用 Docker 和 Github repo 中的 Dockerfile 轻松构建和部署。 这就是我通常在我的服务器上运行我的机器人的方式。

    感谢阅读！ 不要犹豫，分享您对机器人的看法并询问功能😁



原文:

⚠ SOME FEATURES ARE STILL WIP ⚠

Before we get started, I just want to give few words on my relationship with python 😄 (Jump straight to the bot)

Relationship with Python

I've used Python since almost 10 years but I have never deep dive into it, I use it sparingly when I just need to make quick tools like sorting files, handle data, need a quick QT interface for a tool. Even though I've made many small projects thought the years I consider myself a beginner in Python.

Why a StackOverflow Bot ?

Making a Discord bot was a little challenge because many of my friends have already done it. I choose StackOverflow because the StackExchange/StackOverflow API is basically just playing with URL variable and making web request that sends back JSON.

The bot itself

The bot is made using Python 3.9 and Pycord
Github of the project

Question send by the bot

Command to search questions

The bot can currently accept 4 inputs

  • search : the terms of your search
  • tags: tags for the search separated by semicolon
  • sort: sort method for the search (votes, relevance, creation, activity)
  • min: it's used depend on the sorting method, for now this only can be used in votes sort.

By reacting with ⏭, the bot show you the next question !
You can see up to 5 questions

TODO :

  • Add a max option
  • Add the possibility to write a date in min and max so you can use it in creation or activity

Containerize my bot 🐋

My bot can be easily build and deploy using the Docker with the Dockerfile in the Github repo. That's how I usually run my bot on my server.

Thank for reading ! Don't hesitate to share your opinion about the bot and ask for features 😁
Items.Author: alanparadis

Items.Title: Nx NestJs - 如何自动生成 OpenApi/Swagger 规范 | Nx NestJs - How to autogenerate OpenApi/Swagger specs
Items.Description:
大家好，这次是短文。
当我开始使用带有 Nx 的 NestJs 时，我注意到了一个意外点，关于如何使用自动文档选项在 NestJs 中激活 OpenApi/Swagger 仍然存在争议。

不要再害怕了，在本教程结束时，这个问题将得到解决。

使用的版本

在撰写本文时，我使用的是最新的 Nx，即 V14，带有 NestJs 8.0 和 NestJs Swagger 5.2。

套餐

首先安装所需的包，如果你使用 express install npm install --save @nestjs/swagger swagger-ui-express否则，对于 fastify 使用npm install --save @nestjs/swagger fastify-swagger

添加 Swagger 模块

在main.ts项目文件夹的 main.ts 中添加以下内容：

 function setupOpenApi ( app : INestApplication ) { const config = new DocumentBuilder (). setTitle ( ' API Documentation ' ). setVersion ( ' 1.0 ' ). addTag ( ' api ' ). build (); const document = SwaggerModule . createDocument ( app , config ); SwaggerModule . setup ( ' api ' , app , document ); }

然后在bootstrap函数中调用setupOpenApi函数。

我的整个main.ts看起来像这样，请注意我喜欢以下选项：

  • 我使用了 Fastify，因为我对 express 中间件没有任何硬依赖，它提高了性能
  • 我使用了全局前缀，因此我必须在上面的函数中设置useGlobalPrefix:true
  • 我使用的全局前缀是与 OpenApi 的默认路由冲突的默认api ，在文档中恰好是api 。 如果您在不添加useGlobalPrefix: true标志的情况下保留它们，那么您将能够访问http://localhost:3000/api上的 API 和 OpenAPI，但您会注意到文档是错误的，因为它没有包含使用的globalPrefix 。 为了解决这个问题，我们必须为 OpenAPI 设置 useGlobalPrefix useGlobalPrefix: true标志并访问http://localhost:3000/api/api的文档，这看起来很奇怪，所以我将它自定义为http://localhost:3000/api/openApi工作示例如下：
 /** * This is not a production server yet! * This is only a minimal backend to get started. */ import { INestApplication , Logger } from ' @nestjs/common ' ; import { NestFactory } from ' @nestjs/core ' ; import { FastifyAdapter , NestFastifyApplication } from ' @nestjs/platform-fastify ' ; import { DocumentBuilder , SwaggerModule } from ' @nestjs/swagger ' ; import { AppModule } from ' ./app/app.module ' ; async function bootstrap () { const fastifyOptions : ConstructorParameters < typeof FastifyAdapter > [ 0 ] = { logger : true , }; const fastifyAdapter = new FastifyAdapter ( fastifyOptions ); const app = await NestFactory . create < NestFastifyApplication > ( AppModule , fastifyAdapter ); const globalPrefix = ' api ' ; app . setGlobalPrefix ( globalPrefix ); setupOpenApi ( app ); const port = process . env . PORT || 3333 ; await app . listen ( port ); Logger . log ( `🚀 Application is running on: http://localhost: ${ port } / ${ globalPrefix } ` ); } bootstrap (); function setupOpenApi ( app : INestApplication ) { const config = new DocumentBuilder (). setTitle ( ' API Documentation ' ). setVersion ( ' 1.0 ' ). addTag ( ' api ' ). build (); const document = SwaggerModule . createDocument ( app , config ); SwaggerModule . setup ( ' openApi ' , app , document , { useGlobalPrefix : true }); }

现在你已经准备好使用包的手动文档部分了，但我们都知道你不是为了那个

汽车文档

这将是相当短的，我们将有 2 种非 hacky 的方法来做到这一点。

只需在项目根目录下创建一个nest-cli.json文件，然后添加

{ "collection" : "@nestjs/schematics" , "sourceRoot" : "apps" , "compilerOptions" : { "plugins" : [ { "name" : "@nestjs/swagger/plugin" , "options" : { "dtoFileNameSuffix" : [ ".entity.ts" , ".dto.ts" ], "controllerFileNameSuffix" : [ ".controller.ts" ], "classValidatorShim" : true , "dtoKeyOfComment" : "description" , "controllerKeyOfComment" : "description" , "introspectComments" : true } } ] }
}

不要被上述设置吓到，它们只是更明确的默认设置。 真正改变的是sourceRoot ，在 NX 的情况下（至少对于带有应用程序的预设）是文件夹apps 。

这应该会检测到您的参数和类名，但很可能看不到您的类中的内容，并且它们将显示为空。

转到 NestJs 项目文件夹中的project.json或根文件夹中的angular.json以获取旧版本，然后搜索您需要的项目。 我们将需要更改build命令。

整个构建命令如下所示

 "build" : { "executor" : "@nrwl/node:webpack" , "outputs" : [ "{options.outputPath}" ], "options" : { "outputPath" : "dist/apps/api" , "main" : "apps/api/src/main.ts" , "tsConfig" : "apps/api/tsconfig.app.json" , "tsPlugins" : [ { "name" : "@nestjs/swagger/plugin" , "options" : { "dtoFileNameSuffix" : [ ".entity.ts" , ".dto.ts" ], "controllerFileNameSuffix" : [ ".controller.ts" ], "classValidatorShim" : true , "dtoKeyOfComment" : "description" , "controllerKeyOfComment" : "description" , "introspectComments" : true } } ], "assets" : [ "apps/api/src/assets" ] },

如您所见，我们添加了一个自定义 Webpack TsPlugin，如https://docs.nestjs.com/openapi/cli-plugin#integration-with-ts-jest-e2e-tests上面的文档中指定的那样。

如前所述，这是推荐的方法，只要将它们导入项目中，它将从 monorepo 中的任何位置查看所有类。

最终细节

请记住以下几点：

  • 您只能使用class es 而不是interface es
  • 你需要附加一个.dto.ts或.entity.ts除非你改变它

谢谢阅读！



原文:

Hello everyone, short article this time.
As I started using NestJs with Nx I noticed a point of contingency, there are still debates on how to activate OpenApi/Swagger in NestJs with the auto documentation option.

Fear no more, at the end of this tutorial that problem will be solved.

Versions used

I am using the latest Nx at the time of writing, which is V14, with NestJs 8.0 and NestJs Swagger 5.2.

Packages

Install the required package first, if you use express install npm install --save @nestjs/swagger swagger-ui-express otherwise, for fastify use npm install --save @nestjs/swagger fastify-swagger

Add Swagger Module

In main.ts from your NestJs' project folder add the following:

function setupOpenApi(app: INestApplication) { const config = new DocumentBuilder().setTitle('API Documentation').setVersion('1.0').addTag('api').build(); const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config); SwaggerModule.setup('api', app, document);
}

then in the bootstrap function just call the setupOpenApi function.

My entire main.ts looks like this, take note that I preffered the following options:

  • I used Fastify, because I don't have any hard dependency on an express middleware and it improves the performance
  • I Used a global prefix, thus the I had to set useGlobalPrefix:true in the function above
  • The global prefix I used is the default api that conflicts with the default route for OpenApi which in the documentation is coincidently api. If you keep them like this without adding the useGlobalPrefix: true flag then you will be able to acces both the APIs and the OpenAPI on http://localhost:3000/api but you will notice that the documentation is wrong as it doesn't contain the globalPrefix used. To fix this we will have to set the useGlobalPrefix: true flag for OpenAPI and access the docmentation at http://localhost:3000/api/api which looks weird so I customized it to http://localhost:3000/api/openApi Working example bellow: 
/** * This is not a production server yet! * This is only a minimal backend to get started. */
import { INestApplication, Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
import { NestFactory } from '@nestjs/core';
import { FastifyAdapter, NestFastifyApplication } from '@nestjs/platform-fastify';
import { DocumentBuilder, SwaggerModule } from '@nestjs/swagger'; import { AppModule } from './app/app.module'; async function bootstrap() { const fastifyOptions: ConstructorParameters<typeof FastifyAdapter>[0] = { logger: true, }; const fastifyAdapter = new FastifyAdapter(fastifyOptions); const app = await NestFactory.create<NestFastifyApplication>(AppModule, fastifyAdapter); const globalPrefix = 'api'; app.setGlobalPrefix(globalPrefix); setupOpenApi(app); const port = process.env.PORT || 3333; await app.listen(port); Logger.log(`🚀 Application is running on: http://localhost:${port}/${globalPrefix}`);
} bootstrap(); function setupOpenApi(app: INestApplication) { const config = new DocumentBuilder().setTitle('API Documentation').setVersion('1.0').addTag('api').build(); const document = SwaggerModule.createDocument(app, config); SwaggerModule.setup('openApi', app, document, { useGlobalPrefix: true });
}

Right now you are ready to use the manual documentation part of the package, but we both know you are not here for that

Auto Documentation

This will be rather short, we will have 2, non-hacky, ways to do this.

Nest-Cli.json - Not suggested

Just create a nest-cli.json file at the root of the project, then add

{ "collection": "@nestjs/schematics", "sourceRoot": "apps", "compilerOptions": { "plugins": [ { "name": "@nestjs/swagger/plugin", "options": { "dtoFileNameSuffix": [".entity.ts", ".dto.ts"], "controllerFileNameSuffix": [".controller.ts"], "classValidatorShim": true, "dtoKeyOfComment": "description", "controllerKeyOfComment": "description", "introspectComments": true } } ] }
}

Do not be scared from the above settings, they are just the defaults to be more explicit. What is really changed is the sourceRoot which in the case on NX (at least for the presets with apps) is the folder apps.

This should detect your parameters, and class names, but will most likely not see what is inside your classes and they will appear empty.

TsPlugin - Recommended, more customizable

Go to your project.json in your NestJs' project folder or angular.json in the root folder for older version and search for the project you need. We will need to alter the build command.

The entire build command is displayed bellow

 "build": { "executor": "@nrwl/node:webpack", "outputs": ["{options.outputPath}"], "options": { "outputPath": "dist/apps/api", "main": "apps/api/src/main.ts", "tsConfig": "apps/api/tsconfig.app.json", "tsPlugins": [ { "name": "@nestjs/swagger/plugin", "options": { "dtoFileNameSuffix": [".entity.ts", ".dto.ts"], "controllerFileNameSuffix": [".controller.ts"], "classValidatorShim": true, "dtoKeyOfComment": "description", "controllerKeyOfComment": "description", "introspectComments": true } } ], "assets": ["apps/api/src/assets"] },

As you can notice we added a custom Webpack TsPlugin as specified in the documentation above this https://docs.nestjs.com/openapi/cli-plugin#integration-with-ts-jest-e2e-tests.

As mentioned, this is the recommended approach, it will see all the classes from anywhere in the monorepo as long as they are imported in the project.

Final details

Keep in mind the following:

  • You can use only classes not interfacees
  • You need to append a .dto.ts or .entity.ts unless you change that

Thanks for reading!
Items.Author: ipreda

Items.Title: SpriteKit 中更好的时间管理 | Better Time Management in SpriteKit
Items.Description:
时间管理和增量时间的使用是大多数游戏项目最重要和最基础的部分之一，因为它确保任何屏幕转换的一致性，无论游戏运行的设备帧速率是多少。

或多或少任何形式的移动、旋转或与时间相关的计算都应考虑增量时间，以确保它不依赖于帧速率。

例如，在不使用增量时间的情况下，游戏中的子弹在 120 fps 设备上的移动速度与 60 fps 设备上的移动速度不同。

并不是 Apple 的游戏框架工程师不知道 delta time，因为 GameplayKit 中的GKComponent在其update(deltaTime:)方法中需要 delta time。 但是你必须自己计算价值。

同时遗憾的是 SpriteKit 没有提供开箱即用的 delta 时间，GameplayKit 工程师在调用 update 方法时认为deltaTime是一个要求也是可以理解的，因为一个组件很可能需要在一个组件中使用 delta 时间方式或其他。

当然，如果不使用 GameplayKit 而只使用普通的 SpriteKit，则无论设备以何种帧速率运行，您都需要使用增量时间进行屏幕计算，以确保一致性。

在这个SKScene的生命周期中，我们得到了 update 方法中提供的当前时间。 通过将当前时间值存储在类属性lastUpdateTime中，我们能够跟踪自上次调用 update 方法以来已经过去了多少时间，并由此获得了我们的增量时间值。

然后我们可以获取这个值并继续将其传递给需要使用增量时间进行计算的其他方法或系统。

这可能看起来像这样。

 override func update ( _ currentTime : TimeInterval ) { super . update ( currentTime ) let deltaTime = calculateDeltaTime ( from : currentTime ) // Update the bullet system that handle movement of all bullets on screen. bulletSystem . update ( deltaTime : deltaTime ) // Update each GameplayKit component system. for componentSystem in componentSystems { componentSystem . update ( deltaTime : deltaTime ) } }

然后，每个使用 delta 时间的系统、组件或对象都必须接受其方法签名中的值，然后将其沿链进一步传递给依赖 delta 时间的自己的方法。 您很可能不想将所有逻辑保留在更新方法中，因为它可能会变得庞大，而是在专用方法中构建逻辑。

然后我们最终得到这样的组件。

 class MoveComponent : GKComponent { // ... Initialization and references to other components of the entity here ... override func update ( deltaTime seconds : TimeInterval ) { super . update ( deltaTime : seconds ) move ( deltaTime : seconds ) } private funct move ( deltaTime seconds : TimeInterval ) { entity . position += speed * direction * CGFloat ( seconds ) } }

这里我们在组件中有一个 move 方法，它以不依赖于设备帧速率的速度和方向移动实体的位置，因为我们将结果速度乘以我们的增量时间值。 由于增量时间作为TimeInterval传递，我们必须将其转换为适当的格式，例如CGFloat 。

大多数游戏当然不仅会使用 SKScene 中的 update 方法，还需要使用生命周期中的其他方法，例如didSimulatePhysics或didFinishUpdate 。

这些方法不会注入当前时间，因此我们必须将deltaTime属性提升为类属性，以便能够在这些场景中访问和使用 delta 时间。

 class GameplayScene : SKScene { var deltaTime : TimeInterval = 0 override func update ( _ currentTime : TimeInterval ) { deltaTime = calculateDeltaTime ( from : currentTime ) } override func didSimulatePhysics () { // Update camera movemement. camera ? . entity ? . update ( deltaTime : deltaTime ) // Update each 'after physics' component system. for componentSystem in afterPhysicsComponentSystems { componentSystem . update ( deltaTime : deltaTime ) } } }

虽然这是一种可以完成工作的方法，并且 SpriteKit 为我们提供了处理增量时间的必要数据，但它非常复杂。

我们将时间逻辑与场景逻辑混合在一起。 由于每一个影响屏幕上发生的事情的计算（在大多数游戏中都很常见）都依赖于增量时间，因此我们将不得不在系统、类和方法之间不断传递值。

我们也可能有多个场景，甚至同时运行，这在场景之间转换时会发生，这会同时创建多个增量时间计算。

游戏变得越复杂，系统和类越多，这种方法就越会开始分崩离析。 让我们找到更好的方法。

游戏引擎

看看 Unity 和 Unreal 等现代游戏引擎，他们将增量时间计算为核心功能，并且可以在您需要的地方使用该值。 任何以游戏开发为目标的框架都可以实现这一点，这是有道理的。

例如，如果我们仔细看看 Unity，它提供了一个 Time 类1 ，它具有包含时间相关数据的静态属性。

这样，您就不必在游戏代码中传递任何时间信息。 相反，您可以随时通过静态访问Time的deltaTime属性来进行这样的计算。

 var position += speed * direction * Time . deltaTime ;

很明显，他们决定在游戏开发中如此重要且经常使用的东西应该在后台进行计算，并且随时可以轻松使用，这使得它可以静态解决。

静态时间的 Swift 类

我们现在研究了在 SpriteKit 中处理增量时间的普通方法，并了解了现代游戏引擎如何处理它。 因此，让我们瞄准 SpriteKit 中的类似方法，以便更好地通过静态属性轻松访问时间信息。

我们将从 Unity 中获取灵感，看看我们如何在 SpriteKit 中提供一个Time类，该类具有与当前场景的更新循环无关的静态属性。

静态类需要将相关属性与update()方法一起保存以保持值最新。

 /// Tracks the game's time-related information. public enum Time { /// The time given at the beginning of this frame. public private(set) static var time = TimeInterval ( 0.0 ) /// The interval in seconds from the last frame to the current one. public private(set) static var deltaTime = TimeInterval ( 0.0 ) /// Called on the device's frame update to track time properties. static func update ( at currentTime : TimeInterval ) { // If `time` is 0.0 the game has just started, so set it to `currentTime`. if time . isZero { time = currentTime } // Calculate delta time since `update(at:)` was last called. deltaTime = currentTime - time // Set `time` to `currentTime for next game loop update. time = currentTime } }

这为我们提供了具有静态属性的Time类和使它们保持最新的方法。 现在我们只需要一个地方来调用update()方法。 第一个想法可能是从SKScene的update()中调用它。 大部分时间都可以。

如果我们从SKScene执行此操作，我们确实会遇到一个问题，有时我们可能会运行多个场景，例如在两个场景之间转换时。 当他们都更新Time ，这将导致竞争条件，这将为其中一个场景提供不正确的deltaTime 。

我们还希望保持我们的代码干净，并且不必记住在我们创建的每个场景类中调用Time.update() ，并且我们希望避免用不必存在的代码污染场景类。

幸运的是SKView有一个委托属性，我们可以使用它。 让我们创建我们自己的Ticker类，它是一个SKViewDelegate以便我们可以更新我们的Time类。 最后，我们需要在游戏初始化过程中将委托分配给 SKView。

我更喜欢有一个GameManager类，我可以在其中进行所有早期初始化。 我立即从我的ViewController中实例化我的GameManager ，在那里我传入SKView ，它将呈现我的不同游戏场景。

这是分配自定义委托的最佳位置，这将确保时间属性保持最新，而无需在每个场景的基础上进行管理。

 class GameManager { /// Ticker that updates game time properties. let ticker = Ticker () init ( view : SKView ) { // Assign the game time ticker to the view. view . delegate = ticker // ... Other game setup code would go here... } }

我们有了它，一个坚如磐石的可重用解决方案，让我们可以从游戏代码中的任何地方轻松访问时间属性，如增量时间。

每当我们需要进行基于增量时间的计算时，我们现在可以随时使用Time.deltaTime作为计算的一部分。

进一步的改进

有了时间属性的中心位置，它不必以增量时间结束。 有很多与时间相关的属性对游戏开发很有用。 如果我们将 Unity 的Time类作为参考，我们可以在那里复制更多属性并继续装饰我们自己的Time类。

假设我们还想访问游戏代码中的帧数。

 public enum Time { /// The total number of frames since the start of the game. public private(set) static var frameCount = 0 static func update ( at currentTime : TimeInterval ) { // ... previous code in the update method here ... // Increase frame count since game started. frameCount += 1 } }

如果我们需要读取这个值，我们现在可以随时访问Time.frameCount 。

最后的调整，让我们扩展我们的值类型运算符来处理TimeInterval 。

之前在这篇文章中，我们将速度与增量时间相乘，并且必须将时间值转换为 CGFloat, CGFloat(seconds) 。 由于我们将在许多地方使用 delta 时间值，如果我们不必每次都强制转换它会很方便。 相反，我们可以扩展我们将要使用增量时间的类型，例如CGFloat和CGVector以使用TimeInterval 。

这是一个扩展CGFloat的示例，因此我们可以直接将CGFloat值与TimeInterval相乘，而不必每次都强制转换它。

 public extension CGFloat { static func * ( lhs : CGFloat , rhs : TimeInterval ) -> CGFloat { return lhs * CGFloat ( rhs ) } }

这样，我们现在就可以像这样编写干净的游戏代码了。

 movement = speed * Time . deltaTime

真漂亮！



原文:

Time management and the usage of delta time is one of the most important and fundamental parts for most game projects as it ensures consistency of any on-screen transforms no matter what the device's frame rate is that the game runs on.

More or less any form of movement, rotation or time-related calculation should take delta time into account to ensure it's not dependent on the frame rate.

Without using delta time, a bullet in a game, as an example, would move at a different speed on a 120 fps device compared to a 60 fps device.

Delta Time in SpriteKit

SpriteKit does not give us a delta time value out of the box, which is unfortunate considering how useful it is.

It's not that the game framework engineers at Apple are not aware of delta time, as the GKComponent in GameplayKit requires delta time in its update(deltaTime:) method. But you have to calculate the value yourself.

At the same time as it's unfortunate that SpriteKit does not provide delta time out of the box, it's also understandable that the GameplayKit engineers deemed deltaTime as a requirement when calling the update method, as a component will most likely need to use delta time in one way or another.

And of course, if not using GameplayKit but only plain SpriteKit, you are going to need to use a delta time for your on-screen calculations to ensure consistency no matter what frame rate the device runs on.

SKScene provides the current time value, so most likely you are using or have seen solutions similar to this to get the delta time value.

class GameplayScene: SKScene { /// Keeps track of how much time has passed since last game loop update. var lastUpdateTime: TimeInterval = 0 override func update(_ currentTime: TimeInterval) { super.update(currentTime) // Get delta time since last time `update` was called. let deltaTime = calculateDeltaTime(from: currentTime) } /// Calculates time passed from current time since last update time. private func calculateDeltaTime(from currentTime: TimeInterval) -> TimeInterval { // When the level is started or after the game has been paused, the last update time is reset to the current time. if lastUpdateTime.isZero { lastUpdateTime = currentTime } // Calculate delta time since `update` was last called. let deltaTime = currentTime - lastUpdateTime // Use current time as the last update time on next game loop update. lastUpdateTime = currentTime return deltaTime }
}

In the life cycle of this SKScene we get the current time provided in the update method. By storing the current time value in the class property lastUpdateTime, we are able to keep track of how much time that has elapsed since the last time the update method was called, and by that we have our delta time value.

We can then take this value and keep passing it along to other methods or systems that need to use delta time for their calculations.

Which could look something like this.

override func update(_ currentTime: TimeInterval) { super.update(currentTime) let deltaTime = calculateDeltaTime(from: currentTime) // Update the bullet system that handle movement of all bullets on screen. bulletSystem.update(deltaTime: deltaTime) // Update each GameplayKit component system. for componentSystem in componentSystems { componentSystem.update(deltaTime: deltaTime) }
}

Each system, component or object that uses delta time will then have to accept the value in its method signature, and then pass it along further down the chain to its own methods that relies on delta time. You most likely will not want to keep all your logic in the update method, as it could grow massive, but structure the logic in dedicated methods.

We then end up with components like this.

class MoveComponent: GKComponent { // ... Initialization and references to other components of the entity here ... override func update(deltaTime seconds: TimeInterval) { super.update(deltaTime: seconds) move(deltaTime: seconds) } private funct move(deltaTime seconds: TimeInterval) { entity.position += speed * direction * CGFloat(seconds) }
}

Here we have a move method in the component that moves the entity's position at a speed and direction that is not dependent on the device frame rate as we multiply the resulting velocity with our delta time value. As delta time is passed as a TimeInterval we have to cast it to an appropriate format, like CGFloat.

Most games will of course not only use the update method in SKScene, but will need to use the other methods in the life cycle, such as didSimulatePhysics or didFinishUpdate.

Those methods do not get the current time injected, so we are going to have to promote the deltaTime property to a class property to be able to access and use delta time in those scenarios.

class GameplayScene: SKScene { var deltaTime: TimeInterval = 0 override func update(_ currentTime: TimeInterval) { deltaTime = calculateDeltaTime(from: currentTime) } override func didSimulatePhysics() { // Update camera movemement. camera?.entity?.update(deltaTime: deltaTime) // Update each 'after physics' component system. for componentSystem in afterPhysicsComponentSystems { componentSystem.update(deltaTime: deltaTime) } }
}

While this is an approach that does the job, and SpriteKit provides us with the necessary data to deal with delta time, it's quite convoluted.

We are mixing the time logic with the scene logic. And as every calculation that affects what happens on the screen, which is a lot in most games, relies on delta time, we are going to have to keep passing the value around between systems, classes and methods.

We might also have multiple scenes, even running at the same time, which happens when transitioning between scenes, which would create multiple delta time calculations at the same time.

The more complex a game becomes, with more systems and classes, the more this approach will feel to start falling apart. Let's find a better way.

Game Engines

Looking at modern game engines such as Unity and Unreal, they calculate delta time as a core functionality and the value is made available to use where you need it. It makes sense that any framework that targets game development makes this available.

If we for instance take a closer peek at Unity, it provides a Time class1 that has static properties containing time-related data.

By that, you never have to pass any time information around in your game code. Instead, you can at any time do a calculation like this, by just statically accessing the deltaTime property of Time.

var position += speed * direction * Time.deltaTime;

It's apparent that they decided that something so crucial and that is used so often in game development should be calculated under the hood and be easily available at any time, which making it static solves.

The time class in Unity does not only provide deltaTime as a static property but has a ton of different useful time-related information.

Swift Class for Static Time

We have now examined the ordinary approach to dealing with delta time in SpriteKit as well as taken a peek at how modern game engines handle it. So let's aim for a similar approach in SpriteKit for a better way to easily get access to time information via static properties.

We'll take inspiration from Unity and see how we can have a Time class available in SpriteKit with static properties that are not tied to the current scene's update loop.

The static class will need to hold relevant properties together with an update() method to keep the values current.

/// Tracks the game's time-related information.
public enum Time { /// The time given at the beginning of this frame. public private(set) static var time = TimeInterval(0.0) /// The interval in seconds from the last frame to the current one. public private(set) static var deltaTime = TimeInterval(0.0) /// Called on the device's frame update to track time properties. static func update(at currentTime: TimeInterval) { // If `time` is 0.0 the game has just started, so set it to `currentTime`. if time.isZero { time = currentTime } // Calculate delta time since `update(at:)` was last called. deltaTime = currentTime - time // Set `time` to `currentTime for next game loop update. time = currentTime }
}

That gives us the Time class with the static properties and a method to keep them up to date. Now we just need a place to call the update() method. The first thought might be to call it from update() in SKScene. Which would work most of the time.

We do get one problem if we do it from SKScene, at some times we might have more than one scene running, for instance when transitioning between two scenes. That would cause a race condition when then they would both update Time, which will give us an incorrect deltaTime for one of the scenes.

We also want to keep our code clean and not have to remember to call Time.update() in every scene class we create, as well as we want to avoid polluting the scene class with code that doesn't have to be there.

Luckily SKView has a delegate property that we can use for this. The delegate property takes an SKViewDelegate2 object, which gives us the hook we need to have a place to calculate time values outside the scene class.

Let's create our own Ticker class, which is an SKViewDelegate so we can update our Time class. The view() method in SKViewDelegate is called just before the update() method in SKScene, so it's a perfect place for us to use to update time properties.

/// Assign to the `SKView` to update game time properties for each frame.
public class Ticker: NSObject, SKViewDelegate { public func view(_ view: SKView, shouldRenderAtTime time: TimeInterval) -> Bool { // Update time properties. Time.update(at: time) // By returning true the game runs at the full frame rate specified with preferredFramesPerSecond. return true }
}

And finally, we need to assign the delegate to the SKView, during the game's initialization process.

I prefer to have a GameManager class where I do all my early initialization. I instantiate my GameManager immediately from my ViewController where I pass in the SKView that will present my different game scenes.

That is the perfect spot to assign the custom delegate that will ensure that the time properties stay up to date without having to manage that on a per-scene basis.

class GameManager { /// Ticker that updates game time properties. let ticker = Ticker() init(view: SKView) { // Assign the game time ticker to the view. view.delegate = ticker // ... Other game setup code would go here... }
}

And there we have it, a rock-solid reusable solution that gives us easy access to time properties like delta time from anywhere in our game code.

Whenever we need to do a delta time based calculation, we can now at any time use Time.deltaTime as part of the calculation.

Further Improvements

With a central location for our time properties, it doesn't have to end with delta time. There are plenty of time-related properties that are useful for game development. If we look at Unity's Time class as a reference, we could replicate more of the properties there and keep decorating our own Time class.

Let's say we also want to have access to the frame count in our game code.

public enum Time { /// The total number of frames since the start of the game. public private(set) static var frameCount = 0 static func update(at currentTime: TimeInterval) { // ... previous code in the update method here ... // Increase frame count since game started. frameCount += 1 }
}

We can now at any time access Time.frameCount if we need to read this value.

The final tweak, let's extend our value types operators to handle TimeInterval.

Previously in this post we multiplied speed with delta time and had to cast the time value to CGFloat, CGFloat(seconds). As we are going to use the delta time value in many places, it would be convenient if we didn't have to cast it every time. Instead, we can extend types we are going to use with delta time, such as CGFloat and CGVector to use TimeInterval.

Here's an example of extending CGFloat so we can multiply a CGFloat value directly with a TimeInterval without having to cast it every time.

public extension CGFloat { static func * (lhs: CGFloat, rhs: TimeInterval) -> CGFloat { return lhs * CGFloat(rhs) }
}

By that, we are now able to write clean game code like this.

movement = speed * Time.deltaTime

What a beauty!

Items.Title: 在您成功之前，没有人会听到您的创业故事。 | No one will hear your startup story until you are successful.
